evidences.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Mexico City is the only capital of the three host nations chosen as a venue site; Ottawa and Washington, D.C., joined Bonn (West Germany, 1974) and Tokyo (Japan, 2002) as the only capital cities not selected to host World Cup matches. Washington was a host city candidate, but the poor state of Northwest Stadium caused the city to combine its bid with nearby Baltimore's M&T Bank Stadium, which was unsuccessful. Other cities eliminated from the final hosting list were Cincinnati, Denver, Nashville, Orlando, and Edmonton. Ottawa's candidate venue, TD Place Stadium, was eliminated early on for insufficient capacity. evidences.title: How 2026 World Cup Works: Format, Rules and What You Need to Know
evidences.content: ## FAQs: Understanding the 2026 World Cup ### When does the 2026 World Cup take place? The tournament will run from June to July 2026. The opening game of the 2026 World Cup is on June 11 at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City. Meanwhile, the Final takes place on July 19 at MetLife Stadium in New Rutherford, New Jersey. Exact match dates and times for many of the matches will be confirmed closer to the tournament's start. ### How many matches will each team play? Every nation plays at least three matches in the group stage. Teams that advance can play up to eight total matches, depending on how far they go. ### Can matches end in a tie? Yes, group-stage matches can end in a draw. Only knockout matches require extra time or penalties to determine a winner. [...] 48 teams will be split into 12 groups of four. The top two teams from each group advance automatically. The eight best third-place teams also qualify for the knockout rounds. That’s 32 teams moving forward and an unprecedented 104 total matches, making 2026 the biggest tournament the sport has ever seen. For fans, that means over an entire month of daily soccer across North America. ## The Group Stage: Building the Drama The gr...

evidences.title: Beginner's guide to 2026 FIFA World Cup: Schedule, format & more
evidences.content: Arrowhead Stadium") ## The format: How does the tournament work? This is the first time the World Cup will feature 48 teams, so the format will be slightly different. It starts with the group stage, then heads to knockout rounds. Group stage: Each of the 48 teams has been split into 12 groups of four teams, decided by the World Cup draw in December. The teams in each group play each other once, meaning all nations will play a minimum of three games at the tournament. The top two teams from each group are guaranteed to advance, as will the eight best third-place teams. Teams finishing fourth in groups are eliminated. [...] Knockout stage: The 32 surviving teams after the group stage will enter a single-elimination tournament, with one exception: The teams that lose in the semifinals will advance to a third-place match the day before the World Cup final. ## Group breakdown The 48 teams have been split up into the following 12 groups. Each group is a mini-league, with final rankings based on the number of points each team gets from its three matches. A win gets you three points, a draw gets you one and a loss gets you absolutely nothing at all. The top two teams in each group will...

evidences.title: World Cup Format and Rules 2026 Explained for India
evidences.content: In the 2026 World Cup format, each group contains four teams. Every team plays the other teams in its group once, meaning each nation plays three group matches. After all group matches are completed, standings are determined by points. The top two teams in each group qualify automatically for the knockout rounds. The new 48-team format also allows the eight best third-placed teams to qualify for the Round of 32. This means some teams can still advance even if they finish third in their group. Group results can also influence outright markets, qualification odds, and World Cup betting odds during the tournament. This is especially relevant for readers comparing group winner or qualification markets during the group stage. [...] ## How Does the World Cup Format Work? The World Cup format starts with a group stage and then moves into knockout matches. In the 2026 tournament, official organisers have confirmed a 48-team format with 12 groups of four teams before the best-performing sides advance into the Round of 32. Each team begins in the group stage, where they play three matches against the other teams in their group. Teams earn points based on results, and the strongest teams m...

evidences.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup knockout stage - Wikipedia
evidences.content: ## Format The knockout stage of the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be contested between 32 teams that qualify from the group stage. Matches in the knockout stage will be played to a finish. If the score of a match is level at the end of 90 minutes of playing time, 30 minutes of extra time#Association_football "Overtime (sports)") will be played. If the scores are still tied, the match will be decided by a penalty shootout "Penalty shoot-out (association football)"). All times listed are local. In the round of 32, four of the group winners played group runners-up. The other eight group winners played advancing third-place teams. The eight remaining group runners-up played against each other. FIFA set out the following schedule for the round of 32:

evidences.title: Fifa World Cup 2026 Format Explained (48 Teams!)
evidences.content: Imagine this. Your favorite team wins two matches, does not even finish second in the group, and still reaches the knockout stage. Sounds confusing? That is because the World Cup has changed. World Cup 2026 is not using the old 32 team system anymore. This one [music] is bigger, messier, and in some ways even more dramatic. So, let's break it down simply. The first big change is the number of teams. Instead of 32 countries, the 2026 World Cup will have 48. That means more nations, more matches, and more chances for surprise teams to make history. Now, here is how the group stage works. [music] The 48 teams are divided into 12 groups, and each group has four teams. Inside each group, every team plays three matches. So, the group stage still feels familiar. Win, draw, lose, three games to [...] stage still feels familiar. Win, draw, lose, three games to prove yourself. But after that, things change. In the old format, only the top two teams from each group moved on, and the tournament jumps straight into the round of 16. Now, the top two teams from each group still qualify, but they are joined by the eight best third-place teams. That creates a brand new knockout round, the round...

evidences.title: Portugal and Colombia headline a competitive Group K field
evidences.content: Portugal Portugal enters the group as the heavy favorites, and for a good reason. Ranked No. 5 in the FIFA world rankings, manager Roberto Martínez has assembled one of the most complete squads in the tournament. Ronaldo leads the attack, but it’s Bruno Fernandes from Manchester United, one of the Premier League’s top chance creators, who drives the team’s rhythm in the midfield. It’s not just these two stars, as the rest of the team is full of talent all over the field with Rúben Dias, Bernardo Silva, João Neves and Vitinha, to highlight just a few. With world-class talent at every position, Portugal is built to go far. Colombia [...] Congo Congo is to make their first World Cup appearance since 1974 and their first ever under the Democratic Republic of the Congo name. Built heavily around players from the French Ligue 1 and the English Premier League, their squad is better than their FIFA ranking suggests. Under head coach Sébastien Desabre, they have grown into one of Africa’s more dangerous squads, built on physicality and athleticism. Newcastle United’s Yoane Wissa leads the attack as a fast striker, while veteran defender Chancel Mbemba anchors the backline. Congo is likel...

evidences.title: World Cup Format and Rules 2026 Explained for India - ToffeeWeb
evidences.content: ## How Does the World Cup Format Work? The World Cup format starts with a group stage and then moves into knockout matches. In the 2026 tournament, official organisers have confirmed a 48-team format with 12 groups of four teams before the best-performing sides advance into the Round of 32. Each team begins in the group stage, where they play three matches against the other teams in their group. Teams earn points based on results, and the strongest teams move into the knockout stage. After the group stage, the tournament moves into single-elimination football. Teams that lose a knockout match are eliminated, while winners continue progressing toward the final. The 2026 World Cup includes: [...] In the 2026 World Cup format, each group contains four teams. Every team plays the other teams in its group once, meaning each nation plays three group matches. After all group matches are completed, standings are determined by points. The top two teams in each group qualify automatically for the knockout rounds. The new 48-team format also allows the eight best third-placed teams to qualify for the Round of 32. This means some teams can still advance even if they finish third in their gr...

Here's how the format will play out: ### What is the group stage format? 48 teams divided into 12 groups of four Each team plays three group opponents in a round-robin style The top two teams in each group advance to the Round of 32 The eight third-place teams across the 12 groups with the most points also advance to the Round of 32 ### How do knockout rounds work? From this stage onward, the tournament will feature single-elimination, playoff matches. Matches consist of two 45-minute halves in regulation. evidences.title: Procedures and pots | Final Draw | FIFA World Cup 2026
evidences.content: To ensure competitive balance, two separate pathways to the semi-finals have been established when developing the match schedule. In order to have a balanced distribution of the teams, the four highest-ranked teams in the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men’s Ranking, when drawn, will have the following constraints: the highest-ranked team (Spain) and the second highest-ranked team (Argentina) will be randomly drawn into opposite pathways, and the same principle will apply to the third (France) and fourth (England) highest-ranked teams. This will ensure that, should they win their groups, the two highest-ranked teams will not meet before the final. Image 5: FIFA World Cup 2026 Final Draw Pathways graphic 1x1. [...] Explore details Image 7: FIFA ## FIFA World Cup 2026 Final Draw procedures in fullFIFA World Cup 2026 Final Draw procedures in full Image 8: ZURICH, SWITZERLAND - NOVEMBER 11: The FIFA World Cup trophy with Canada, Mexico and USA in the centre ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 at the Home of FIFA on November 11, 2024 in Zurich, Switzerland. evidences.title: Colombia's updated FIFA ranking before 2026 World Cup clash with DR Congo - World Soccer Talk
evidences.content: According to the FIFA Men’s World Ranking, Colombia are ranked 12th with 1712.60 points. After their victory over Uzbekistan, they have climbed one spot in the ranking, nearing the top 10. In recent years, they have managed to regain their consistency, compared to their 49th position in June 2011. However, they have yet to match their highest-ever ranking, which was 3rd in July–August 2018. Despite their upgrade in the FIFA ranking spots, Colombia are not the highest-ranked national team in their group at the 2026 World Cup. Portugal currently hold the 7th spot with 1766.74 points. Then, DR Congo are ranked 43rd spot with 1487.18 points. Finally, Uzbekistan rank as the lowest-ranked national team in the group, holding the 58th spot with 1432.84 points.

evidences.title: How Does the 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw Work? Rules, Format, Full Process Explained - Yahoo Sports
evidences.content: Pot 1 teams will be the first to be drawn in random groups, except for the host nations, who know which group they will be in: Mexico (Group A), Canada (Group B), and the United States (Group D). Then, teams from Pot 2, 3, and 4 will be randomly drawn into the groups to complete the 12 groups for the 2026 World Cup. Conductors of the 2026 World Cup Draw will notify fans if there is a constraint if a team is randomly drawn in a group that also includes a team from the same confederation. In case of a constraint, the team will be moved to the next group available. The draw concludes once all 48 teams are assigned a group, with the official schedule of the matches being decided by FIFA on a later date. [...] Soccer image Soccer image ...
